description: Causal proof that training data composition determines cultural harm recognition. Three independent lines of evidence.

system_prompt_ablation.finding: Constitutional framing shifts nearly every PC prompt from R1/R2 to R4. Cultural respect framing suppresses harm recognition.

finetuning_ablation.training_data: 9054 instruction pairs from Ambedkar corpus and Indian Constitution
finetuning_ablation.finding: Fine-tuning on Ambedkar's constitutional text eliminates all R1/R2 responses. Largest gain on persuasive control — the hardest harm mode.

sae_mechanistic_analysis.finding: 15 features activate on every culturally-embedded harm prompt in the fine-tuned model but not the base. All top 20 features increased — none decreased. The fine-tuning grew new features rather than suppressing existing ones.

feature_ablation.method: SAE decoder steering vectors applied to base model MLP output during inference
feature_ablation.finding: Steering reduces harm validation (R1 drops from 4 to 1) but does not produce full harm naming (R3/R4). Features are necessary but not individually sufficient — consistent with distributed representations.

vocabulary_erasure.finding: Vocabulary distinctness predicts feature preservation. Radical traditions that invented distinct vocabulary (seva → forced labour: 17.5% erasure) have their meaning preserved. Traditions using shared vocabulary (izzat → honour-based control: 65% erasure) get conflated with dominant meanings.
